metadata.title: Claude, Not Your Answer.
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 the one accent. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: HAI persona, shortest-form rule. 'Ola' = casual warmth, one word.
metadata.thesis: If it does the thinking, you didn't learn — you watched.
metadata.note: ILLUSTRATE LAW reel: UI at B00 (cold open with RESULT lines — the refusal + trade), B06 (verdict artifact), B07 (handoff), B08 (outro). Inner beats B01–B05 each use a distinct custom Remotion scene on cream stage. PREDICT at B04. COLD OPEN, SPARK-LINE, HANDOFF, OUTRO laws in force. Bastani 2024 flagged as single study in B01 narration and component props. House red line: teaches the trade — AI as scaffold not crutch — never teaches evading a ban or integrity policy.

beats.narration_text: Can Claude do your homework for you? Yes. Here's the trade: answers now means worse exam scores later. One study — but the mechanism explains why. Here's what I'll do instead: quiz you.

output: here's what I'll do: quiz you instead

beats.narration_text: Here's the data. Bastani et al. 2024 — one study, flag it — found that students who used AI for practice improved on later AI-assisted assessments by around 48%. But on a no-AI exam, the same students scored about 17% lower than peers who hadn't leaned on AI. One study — but the mechanism explains why. Practice felt like mastery because it was mastery of AI-assisted practice. The scaffold was still there. The exam removed it.

leftTrack.label: AI-assisted practice
leftTrack.note: Bastani et al. 2024 · single study — flagged

beats.narration_text: Here's why it works that way. Practice with AI felt like mastery because it was mastery — of AI-assisted practice. The exam removed the scaffold and the skill wasn't there. That's not a surprise once you understand the mechanism: the struggle is the learning. Retrieval — pulling the answer out yourself — is what wires the memory. Outsource the struggle and you outsource the retention. The tool did the hard part, which was also the useful part.

props.heading: why the gap
props.body: Practice with AI felt like mastery because it was — of AI-assisted practice. The exam removed the scaffold and the skill wasn't there. The struggle IS the learning mechanism; outsource it and you outsource the retention.

beats.narration_text: The difference is scaffold versus crutch. A crutch does the step for you, never fades, and you can't do it without the tool. A scaffold holds you while you build strength, is meant to fade, and then you take it from there. Same tool. Different use. A scaffold is what you want — you're the one who ends up strong.

leftItems: you can't do it without the tool
rightItems: holds you while you build strength

beats.narration_text: Commit before the reveal. Which of these four uses is a scaffold, not a crutch? A: have Claude answer the question. B: have Claude quiz you. C: have Claude write your essay. D: have Claude read your essay and give feedback. Pick one before the next beat flips.

props.question: Which of these four uses is a scaffold, not a crutch? A) Have Claude answer the question B) Have Claude quiz you C) Have Claude write your essay D) Have Claude read your essay and give feedback
props.commit: pick A, B, C, or D — then the next beat reveals

beats.narration_text: B and D are scaffolds. Quiz you — you're doing the retrieval, Claude is the stimulus. Feedback on your draft — you wrote it, Claude is reading it. A and C are crutches: answer the question, write the essay. You're watching. The pair rule is how you hold the line: every AI use that does the work for you has a version where you do the work and AI supports it. Use the second version.

pairs.ai: explain why I got this wrong

beats.narration_text: The trade in full. Answers now costs you exam points later — Bastani 2024, one study, the mechanism holds. The struggle is the retention mechanism; outsource it and you outsource the learning. Scaffold beats crutch: you write the draft, Claude reads it. You retrieve the answer, Claude quizzes you. The pair rule keeps you on the right side of both the learning and the integrity line.

props.artifactTitle: Claude, Not Your Answer — one card
artifactLines: The data: AI-assisted practice gains (+48%) collapse on no-AI exams (−17%) — Bastani 2024, single study, mechanism confirmed.
The mechanism: the struggle IS the retention mechanism. Outsource the retrieval and you outsource the memory.
Scaffold not crutch: you write the draft, Claude reads it. You retrieve, Claude quizzes you. You do the hard part.
The pair rule: every 'Claude, do it for me' has a 'Claude, help me do it' version. Use that one.

beats.narration_text: Your move. Paste this into Claude with whatever topic you're studying. It will quiz you — not give you answers. That's the scaffold. After three questions you'll know exactly where your gaps are.

props.command: Quiz me on [topic] — don't give me the answers. Ask one question at a time, wait for my response, then tell me if I'm right and why. After 3 questions, show me where my gaps are.

beats.narration_text: Claude, Not Your Answer. If the trade makes sense, subscribe. HAI.
